key_insight: The proposed preconditioned method efficiently resolves elliptic interface problems on unfitted meshes with a reconstructed discontinuous approximation.
engineering_relevance: This approach enhances numerical stability and convergence for complex interface problems in engineering applications.
method: Develops a preconditioned unfitted finite element method using reconstructed discontinuous approximations.
Employs a modified local least squares problem to ensure stability near interfaces.
Constructs an optimal preconditioner based on piecewise constant space.
evidence_signal: Numerical experiments confirm optimal convergence rates and robust efficiency of the proposed method.
watch_item: The methodology's effectiveness may vary with the complexity of the interface and mesh configuration.
paper_title: Preconditioned Discontinuous Galerkin Method for Elliptic Interface Problem on Unfitted Mesh with Reconstructed Discontinuous Approximation
